que: What is the primary purpose of the Wrap widget in Flutter?
options: To display a single child with padding
To overlay widgets on top of each other
To display children in multiple horizontal or vertical runs that automatically wrap

que: What does Wrap do when there is not enough space on the main axis?
options: Automatically wraps children to the next line or column

que: Which parameter of Wrap holds the list of widgets to display?
Which parameter of Wrap controls the direction in which children are arranged?
Which parameter defines the space between children along the main axis?
Which parameter defines the space between lines (runs) of children?
Which parameter controls how children are aligned within a single run?
Which parameter controls how the runs themselves are aligned within the wrap?
what is a good use case for Wrap?
options: Scrollable lists of hundreds of items
Chips, tags, or any content that may overflow a single line

que: what should you adjust to improve the Wrap layout?
Which of the following is listed as something to avoid with Wrap?
options: Using Wrap for simple one-line layouts instead of Row or Column

que: What should you use instead of Wrap for scrollable content?
what does Wrap extend?

A tag cloud where tags wrap to new lines as needed
